DISCOVERY 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT DEBORAH STEPHENS' MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER RESTRICTING DISCOVERY IS DENIED. DEFENDANT DOES NOT SHOW GOOD CAUSE FOR THE PROTECTIVE ORDER AND PLAINTIFF HAS A COMPELLING NEED FOR THE PERSONNEL FILE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO BOTH THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER WELLS FARGO HAD KNOWLEDGE OF MS. STEPHENS' UNFITNESS PRIOR TO HER REHIRE AND WHETHER MS. BOWEN'S MONEY WAS CONVERTED ON APRIL 4, 2008 WHEN THE TRANSFER OF FUNDS WAS MADE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Mar-23-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES GEAGAN, TEL. NO. (707) 939-9593 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARGARET M. SCHNECK OF ROBINSON & WOOD, INC., TEL. NO. (408) 298-7120 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Denied as to both the request for punitive damages and the second cause of action for conversion. There are triable issues whether Wells Fargo had knowledge of Ms. Stephens' unfitness prior to her rehire and whether Ms. Bowen's money was converted on April 4, 2008 when the transfer of funds was made. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =302/hk
